en: a large mass of ice that moves slowly over land
the build-up of snow where more falls than melts
dense, old snow that is not yet glacial ice
glacial erosion where rocks frozen into the ice scrape the valley floor and walls
erosion where ice freezes onto rock and pulls pieces away as the glacier moves
weathering where water enters cracks, freezes, expands and breaks the rock
a armchair-shaped hollow on a mountainside where a glacier begins
a sharp ridge formed between two corries
a sharp pointed mountain peak formed when three or more corries erode backwards
a valley with steep sides and a flat floor, carved out by a glacier
the broken end of a interlocking spur, cut off by a glacier
boulders deposited by glaciers in an area of different underlying rock
unsorted material deposited directly by a glacier
material carried and deposited by a glacier
material deposited along the sides of a glacier
material deposited in the middle of a glacier where two glaciers merge
material deposited at the furthest point a glacier reached
a smooth, rounded, elongated hill of till deposited beneath a glacier
